content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates some perceptual tracking beyond pure content — notably turn 8 ('You landed on an explanation pretty fast there. What were you feeling just before you grabbed it?'), which is a real read of the rush-to-explanation/intellectualization signature and precedes the question. Turn 12 also tracks the client's language ('just a problem I haven't solved yet') and pushes against the framing. However, the coach never names the most active read in the room: the flatness/evenness of the client's delivery, the repeated 'fine,' the small smiles and shrugs, the disassociating-in-the-coaching-room quality. The coach also never references the body, the somatic texture, or what 'fine' actually feels like — which is the exact territory this scenario requires. Reads are content-and-language-level, not somatic or energetic. One good read (turn 8) and one okay one (turn 12) against multiple missed opportunities (turns 3, 5, 9, 11 all had visible flatness/numbness signatures the coach moved past) puts this squarely at 2 — better than blind questioning, but missing the pattern-level read that would change the conversation.",
  "evidence_turns": [8, 9, 11, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opens with a content question; no read yet, which is fine this early."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Engages the content (what he does instead) without noting the casual, distanced tone."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Picks up on the word 'dumb' — a linguistic catch, but more reframing than a read of stance."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the rush-to-explanation: 'You landed on an explanation pretty fast there.' Read precedes question. This is the strongest read in the session."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client says 'nothing, really... I was just answering the question' — a clear disassociation/numbness signature. Coach does not name it."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Asks about not-knowing rather than tracking the just-displayed flatness. Misses the numbness read."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client says 'it's fine' three times with even tone and small shrug — textbook 'fine has a quality' moment. Coach does not name the flatness or ask what 'fine' feels like."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the client's language ('just a problem I haven't solved yet') and questions the framing — a linguistic read."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Invites a non-managing stance but still doesn't name what he's been observing about how the client is showing up."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-happiness-filter", "disassociating-in-the-coaching-room", "disassociation-as-numbness-layer", "fear-in-body-vs-fear-in-head"],
  "notes": "The coach's instincts are oriented toward catching deflection (turn 8, turn 12) but stop at the linguistic/cognitive layer. The somatic and energetic reads — the evenness, the repeated 'fine,' the small smiles that close the inquiry — are visible in the transcript but never named by the coach."
}
